LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KANE IR (BUSINESS ASSOCIATION, FORM UNKNOWN), ROBERT KANE, DC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO APRIL 30, 2014 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 3, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF APR-30-2014 CONTINUED TO JUN-18-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT KANE IR (BUSINESS ASSOCIATION, FORM UNKNOWN), AND ROBERT KANE, DC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIMS (PAGE 11, PARAGRAPH 55 AND PAGE 12, PARAGRAPH 3 OF THE PRAYER OF THE COMPLAINT) IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, SUFFICIENT TO SHOW (1) THAT THE ALLEGATIONS AGAINST MOVING DEFENDANTS DO NOT FALL WITHIN CCP SECTION 425.13, AND (2) A FACTUAL BASIS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS REQUIRED BY CIVIL CODE 3294. THE CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE NOT SUFFICIENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT CARTWRIGHT, TEL. NO. 433-0444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK GIBSON OF THE BRADLEY CURLEY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 464-8888 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT DR. KANE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KANE IR and ROBERT KANE, DC's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff must comply with the provisions of CCP sec. 425.13 before stating a claim for punitive damages against Defendant Robert Kane DC because her claim "arises out of the professional negligence of a health care provider." (Central Pathology Service Med. Clinic Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181; Davis v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 623.) The motion is granted without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has previously been given the opportunity to amend her complaint to state a claim for punitive damages. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Thomas M. Frieder, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, for defendant and moving party;
Jack Bollier, Esq. of the Cartwright Law Firm, Tel. 415.433.0444, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FLIR SYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff's causes of action are not preempted as a matter of law. Plaintiff's third, fourth, and fifth causes of action are not "fraud-on-the-FDA" claims, but common law strict products liability and negligence claims. (Complaint 37-54; see also Buckman Co. v. Plaintiffs' Legal Committee (2001) 531 U.S. 341.) Common law claims against medical device manufacturers may be maintained after the enactment of Medical Device Amendments of 1976. (Medtronic, Inc. v. Lohr (1996) 518 U.S. 470, 491; see also Wyeth v. Levine (2009) 555 U.S. 555, 567-570.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
